content: I'm so excited to be starting my new life as a chef and restaurant owner. I've always loved cooking and trying new recipes, and I'm looking forward to sharing that with my community.
Congratulations on your new venture! That's very exciting news. What kind of restaurant are you planning to open, and what kind of cuisine will you be serving?
I'm planning to open a farm-to-table restaurant, and we'll be serving a mix of seasonal small plates and larger entrees. We'll also have a great selection of craft beers and wines.
That sounds delicious. As a former journalist, do you think your background in writing and storytelling will influence your approach to menu design and restaurant marketing?
Definitely. I think my experience as a journalist will help me to tell the story of our restaurant and the farmers we work with, and to create a compelling narrative around our menu and mission.
That's a great point. Storytelling is such an important part of creating a memorable dining experience. What kind of pets do you have, or are you more of an animal lover in general?
I have a few beehives on my property, and I love beekeeping. It's such a fascinating hobby, and it's great to be able to harvest my own honey and use it in my cooking.
That's amazing. Beekeeping is such a unique and rewarding hobby. Do you have any other pets, or are the bees your main focus?
I also have a few dogs, and they're such great companions. They love to come with me on hikes and help me forage for wild mushrooms.

That sounds like so much fun. It's great that you have such a strong connection with nature and the outdoors. What kind of mushrooms do you like to forage for, and do you have any favorite recipes that feature them?

I love foraging for chanterelles and oyster mushrooms. They're so versatile and delicious, and they add such a great depth of flavor to dishes. I'm actually planning to feature them in a few of our restaurant's menu items.
That sounds wonderful. I'm sure your customers will love the fresh, seasonal ingredients. Do you have any plans for a grand opening event, or is it more of a soft launch?
We're planning to have a grand opening event on June 1st, and we're really excited to share our restaurant with the community. We'll have live music, great food, and plenty of drinks to go around.
That sounds like a fantastic event. I'm sure it'll be a huge success. Do you have any plans for future events or promotions, or are you focusing on getting the restaurant up and running first?

We're planning to host a few wine pairing dinners and cooking classes, and we'll also have a loyalty program to reward our regular customers. We want to create a welcoming and engaging community around our restaurant.
